description_long: This shotgun features a compact, industrial design with a metallic body and a wooden grip. The receiver and barrel are constructed from steel, with a matte gray finish and visible bolts and seams, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The grip is made of dark, textured wood, and a small metallic trigger guard is present beneath the receiver. A cloth or leather wrap is attached to the side of the receiver, possibly for comfort or grip. The barrel is short and stubby, with a large hexagonal nut at the muzzle end, and the top of the receiver has a simple ring sight. The overall construction suggests a handmade or improvised weapon, suitable for close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact, industrial-style shotgun with a steel body, wooden grip, and a short barrel, featuring rugged details and a utilitarian finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the metallic receiver, wooden grip, cloth wrap, and short barrel with a hexagonal muzzle nut.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening, ring sight, and the narrow profile of the shotgun.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same metallic and wooden components, with the cloth wrap and mechanical details visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the receiver, the bolt, and the alignment of the grip and trigger guard.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short length, no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: small, squared trigger guard with a simple, utilitarian shape and no finger grooves
